title: Numerical Methods
description: Locating roots by sign change, fixed-point iteration, the Newton-Raphson method, and the trapezium rule for approximating definite integrals.
objectives: Use a change of sign of a continuous function to locate a root within an interval
Rearrange an equation f(x) = 0 into the form x = g(x) and use fixed-point iteration x_(n+1) = g(x_n), recognising convergence and divergence
Apply the Newton-Raphson method x_(n+1) = x_n - f(x_n)/f'(x_n) to find an improved estimate of a root
Explain why the Newton-Raphson method can fail (e.g. f'(x_n) = 0 or a poor starting value)
Use the trapezium rule to find a numerical estimate for a definite integral, given a table of values or a function
Judge whether the trapezium rule over- or under-estimates an integral based on the curve's convexity
